news.title: Singapore bank DBS to offer tokenised physical gold to retail customers
news.summary: DBS Group, Singapore's biggest bank by assets, said on Thursday it will offer tokenised physical gold ‌to retail customers as demand for the precious metal grows and ‌the city-state pushes to become a gold trading hub.  Gold prices touched a record high of $5,600 an ounce this year amid concerns over inflation, geopolitical tensions and market volatility, but spot gold fell to $4,111.95 on Wednesday, its lowest since ‌March 23 and down 27% ⁠from that peak.  DBS, which is also Southeast Asia's biggest bank, said in a statement that DBS Physical Gold Tokens will ⁠be available through its digibank app in the second half of 2026.
